author: o1xhack
description: Agentic AI chat assistant that reads, edits, and creates notes through natural conversation. Supports Anthropic, OpenAI, and ChatGPT account sign-in. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Yuxiao (o1xhack)
manifest.description: Agentic AI chat assistant that reads, edits, and creates notes through natural conversation. Supports Anthropic, OpenAI, and ChatGPT account sign-in.
manifest.name: Chatting with AI

name: Chatting with AI

releases.author: o1xhack
releases.description:
## Changes
- Tighten API response parsing around Anthropic, OpenAI, and ChatGPT OAuth so requestUrl JSON is handled as unknown and narrowed before use.
- Parse model-list responses through explicit type guards.
- Type the Svelte chat container API used by the ItemView wrapper.
- Normalize tool input values before using them in messages or Obsidian APIs.
- Use FileManager.trashFile() for delete_file to honor the user's deletion preference.
- Narrow UnifiedResponse.stopReason to the concrete supported values.

## Validation
- npx tsc --noEmit
- npm run svelte-check
- npm run build

releases.author: o1xhack
releases.description:
## Changes
- Remove the plugin name from the settings heading to satisfy Obsidian community review guidance.
- Mark the programmatic send-message promise as intentionally ignored and type its optional selection value.

## Validation
- npx tsc --noEmit
- npm run svelte-check
- npm run build

releases.author: o1xhack
releases.description:
## Changes
- Raise the minimum Obsidian version to 1.11.4 to match SecretStorage usage.
- Stop detaching chat leaves during plugin unload so user workspace layout is preserved.
- Replace direct settings headings and inline settings modal styles with Obsidian Setting headings and CSS classes.
- Use the vault configDir instead of hard-coded .obsidian paths for plugin debug and migration data.
- Mark/await previously floating promises and switch simple timers to window.setTimeout.

## Validation
- npx tsc --noEmit
- npm run svelte-check
- npm run build

releases.author: o1xhack
releases.description:
## Changes
- Rename the plugin to Chatting with AI with the community-safe plugin ID chatting-with-ai.
- Add startup migration from the legacy obsidian-chatting plugin folder to chatting-with-ai, preserving user data and clearing the old folder.
- Migrate provider API keys and ChatGPT OAuth credentials to the new SecretStorage keys.
- Update docs, settings labels, debug paths, user agents, and release metadata for the new plugin identity.

## Validation
- npx tsc --noEmit
- npm run svelte-check
- npm run build

releases.author: o1xhack
releases.description:
## Obsidian Chatting v0.1.0

Mobile-first agentic chat for Obsidian. **Three providers**, **14 vault tools**, every secret in the OS keychain.

> This is the active development release. While the project is still pre-stable we keep iterating on **0.1.0 in-place** rather than burning version numbers — re-install via BRAT to pick up the latest fixes.

### Providers

- **Anthropic** — Claude Sonnet / Opus / Haiku via API key. Adaptive thinking, prompt caching, web search.
- **OpenAI** — Codex / GPT via API key. Responses API, reasoning, web search.
- **ChatGPT OAuth** *(experimental)* — sign in with your ChatGPT account, no API key required. Mobile-friendly Device Code Flow. Web search supported.

### Highlights

- 14 vault-native agentic tools: read / edit / create / rename / delete / search / open / properties / backlinks / ask.
- Mobile-first: iOS / Android / desktop, identical experience. Non-streaming over Obsidian `requestUrl()`.
- API keys and OAuth credentials live in the OS keychain via `SecretStorage`. Never in `data.json`, never synced.
- Conversation history persists across Obsidian restarts.
- Coexists peacefully with upstream Obsidian Chat (separate plugin id, view type, CSS prefix).

### Install via BRAT

1. Install [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat) from Community Plugins.
2. **Add Beta plugin** → `o1xhack/obsidian-chatting`
3. Enable **Obsidian Chatting** in Community Plugins.

### ChatGPT OAuth — known good models

The Codex backend behind `chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses` accepts a small fixed set of slugs (mirroring the official Codex CLI's bundled `models.json`):

- `gpt-5.5` *(recommended, default)*
- `gpt-5.4`
- `gpt-5.4-mini`
- `gpt-5.3-codex`
- `gpt-5.2`

The dropdown in settings shows these by default; `Custom...` lets you type any other slug.

### Caveats

- **ChatGPT OAuth is Experimental.** It uses your ChatGPT account session against the ChatGPT/Codex backend (not `api.openai.com`). Availability, quotas, and request shapes may change without notice. The OpenAI API Key provider remains the recommended stable path.
- Tokens authorized on one device do **not** sync to other devices — each device runs Device Code Flow once.
releases.name: v0.1.0 — first standalone release
